subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the main idea and theme of the poem
Identify the poet’s message about humanity and unity
Recognize basic poetic features such as rhyme and repetition
Develop reading comprehension and vocabulary skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Poem Reading
topics_covered: Background of the poet and poem
Poet’s message of equality and peace
teaching_strategy: Teacher reads the poem aloud slowly
Explain the context and meaning of difficult words
student_activities: Underline new words and meanings in notebook

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Theme
topics_covered: Human feelings, similarities, and shared experiences
teaching_strategy: Discuss the central idea in simple language
Give real-life examples of human unity
student_activities: Write the main theme in own words
Share examples of unity and friendship

lesson_plan.title: Rhyme and Repetition
teaching_strategy: Point out rhyming words in each stanza
student_activities: Copy repeated lines and explain meaning

lesson_plan.title: Moral of the Poem
topics_covered: Lesson of universal brotherhood and peace
teaching_strategy: Teacher explains moral in simple terms
Students give examples from daily life
student_activities: Write one sentence about moral learned
Share personal example of helping or respecting others

teaching_aids: Flashcards for new words and meanings
teacher_tip: Focus on making students understand the poet’s message of equality and shared humanity in simple words, and relate it to their own experiences.
